# Spec - {slug}

## Source Idea
- `./docs/workflows/{slug}/idea.md`

## Status
- [Draft | In review | Approved for plan | Superseded]

## Summary
[One-paragraph summary of the user-visible contract. Reference the idea instead of restating its full rationale.]

## Context And Orientation
[Optional. Add only context a later planner would otherwise miss.]

## Problem Statement
- [What problem the spec solves.]

## Goals
- [Goal 1]

## Non-Goals
- [Explicitly excluded scope.]

## Target Users
- [Primary users or audience.]

## Key Scenarios Or User Flows
- [Scenario 1]

## Functional Requirements
- [Requirement 1. Keep user-visible requirements here; implementation mechanics belong in plan.md.]

## Constraints And Guardrails
- [Constraint, guardrail, or policy that affects the user-visible contract.]

## Boundary Conditions And Edge Cases
- [Edge case that affects correctness.]

## Privacy Rules, Business Rules, And User-Facing Routes Or URLs
- [Add only when relevant.]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [Observable user or product outcome that must be true. Keep this compact; do not duplicate every requirement unless it improves planning safety.]

## Observable Acceptance Behavior Or Examples
- [Concrete user-visible example or scenario, not an implementation validation command or test log.]

## Open Questions
- [Product-contract question to resolve before or during planning. Implementation-only questions belong in plan.md.]
